activities_projects.title: Story Summary Writing
activities_projects.objective: Develop the ability to summarize the story
steps: Read the story carefully
Identify the main events in the story
Write a short summary of the story in about 8–10 sentences
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and completeness of summary (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Character Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the personalities of Margie and Tommy
steps: Write about Margie’s feelings toward her school
Write about Tommy’s interest in the old book
Compare their attitudes toward learning

activities_projects.title: Compare Schools Activity
activities_projects.objective: Analyze differences between old schools and future schools
steps: Make two columns titled 'Old Schools' and 'Future Schools'
List at least five differences mentioned in the story
Explain which type of school you prefer

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Builder
steps: Find 8 new words from the story
Write their meanings
Use each word in a meaningful sentence

activities_projects.title: Technology in Education Discussion
activities_projects.objective: Understand the role of technology in learning
steps: Write how technology is used in Margie’s school
Discuss advantages of mechanical teachers
Discuss disadvantages compared to human teachers
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking about technology in education

activities_projects.title: Event Sequencing Activity
activities_projects.objective: Understand the structure of the story
steps: List important events from the story
Arrange them in chronological order
Write one sentence explaining each event

activities_projects.title: Dialogue Writing
steps: Imagine a conversation between Margie and a student from an old school
Write at least 8 lines of dialogue
Include questions about both types of schools
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops creative writing and dialogue skills

activities_projects.title: Theme Identification
activities_projects.objective: Identify the central theme of the story
steps: Think about what the story teaches about education
Write the main theme in 4–5 sentences
Give examples from the story
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands deeper meaning of the story

activities_projects.title: Future Classroom Design
steps: Design a future classroom using technology
Label the important parts such as screens, computers, or robots
Explain your design in 3–4 sentences

activities_projects.title: Opinion Writing
activities_projects.objective: Express personal views about the story
steps: Write your opinion about mechanical teachers
Explain whether you would prefer them or human teachers
Support your answer with reasons
